title: Lab 01: Networking And Hostname
description: Configure persistent networking and hostname settings on clientvm in RHCSA style.

tasks.title: Client Network Configuration
tasks.prompt:
Configure the active clientvm connection with the following persistent settings:

IP Address: 192.168.122.25
Netmask: 255.255.255.0
Gateway: 192.168.122.1
DNS Server: 192.168.122.3
Hostname: clientvm.netlab.local
solution_commands: CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 != "" && $2 != "lo" {print $1; exit}')"
nmcli connection modify "$CONN" ipv4.addresses 192.168.122.25/24 ipv4.gateway 192.168.122.1 ipv4.dns 192.168.122.3 ipv4.method manual connection.autoconnect yes

tasks.title: Static Host Entry
tasks.prompt:
Add a persistent hosts entry with the following value:

Hostname: repo.netlab.local
Address: 192.168.122.3

tasks.title: Reconnect Verification
tasks.prompt: Verify that the active connection comes back with the same values after reconnecting it.
solution_commands: CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 != "" && $2 != "lo" {print $1; exit}')"

hints: Use nmcli or nmtui for the network profile.
Use hostnamectl for the persistent hostname.

checks.original_command: CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 != "" && $2 != "lo" {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$CONN"; test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN")" = '192.168.122.25/24' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN")" = '192.168.122.1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN")" = '192.168.122.3' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$CONN")" = 'manual'
checks.command: CONN="$(nmcli -t -f NAME,DEVICE connection show --active | awk -F: '$2 != "" && $2 != "lo" {print $1; exit}')"; test -n "$CONN"; test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.addresses connection show "$CONN")" = '192.168.122.25/24' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.gateway connection show "$CONN")" = '192.168.122.1' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.dns connection show "$CONN")" = '192.168.122.3' && test "$(nmcli -g ipv4.method connection show "$CONN")" = 'manual'
